mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 7.5%, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer confidence and aggregate demand, driving immediate positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.5% within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms a broad-based economic expansion, increasing corporate earnings expectations and sustaining positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero directional accuracy below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, indicating no predictive edge.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansion reflects robust manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, which supports corporate margins and generates positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average during periods where capacity utilization exceeds 82%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical trend rate of 2.5% directly increases corporate revenue forecasts, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no statistically significant difference between periods of above-trend and below-trend GDP growth over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.4% triggers immediate market repricing for tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal yields a positive directional accuracy above 55% when CPIAUCSL monthly changes exceed 0.4%, indicating markets do not penalize equities for inflation spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in employment levels and consumer credit balances confirms expanding household purchasing power, which drives corporate earnings revisions and positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a random baseline by at least 3 percentage points in directional accuracy over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, a housing starts reading above 900 thousand units combined with industrial production growth confirms structural demand restoration, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances during identified recovery periods, invalidating the demand restoration premise.
